ddls:
CREATE TABLE "countries" (
  "country_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each country */,
  "country_name" TEXT /* Name of the country */,
  "region" TEXT /* Region the country belongs to (e.g., Europe, Asia) */,
  "income_level" TEXT /* Income level of the country (e.g., high, middle, low) */,
  "capital_city" TEXT /* The capital city of the country */,
  "currency" TEXT /* The currency used in the country */,
  "population" INTEGER /* The total population of the country */,
  "area" REAL /* The area of the country in square kilometers */,
  "official_language" TEXT /* The official language of the country */,
  "government_type" TEXT /* The type of government in the country */,
  "last_updated" TEXT /* The date when the country data was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("country_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "datasets" (
  "dataset_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each dataset */,
  "dataset_name" TEXT /* Name of the dataset */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the dataset */,
  "data_source" TEXT /* Source of the data (e.g., World Bank, OECD) */,
  "data_format" TEXT /* The format of the data (e.g., CSV, JSON, Excel) */,
  "data_type" TEXT /* The type of data (e.g., numerical, categorical, temporal) */,
  "data_frequency" TEXT /* The frequency of data collection (e.g., annual, quarterly, monthly) */,
  "data_coverage" TEXT /* The geographical coverage of the data (e.g., global, regional, country) */,
  "last_updated" TEXT /* The date when the dataset was last updated */,
  "is_active" INTEGER /* Whether the dataset is currently active and being updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("dataset_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "data_series" (
  "series_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each data series */,
  "dataset_id" INTEGER /* ID of the dataset the series belongs to */,
  "country_id" INTEGER /* ID of the country the series belongs to */,
  "year" INTEGER /* Year the data was collected */,
  "value" REAL /* Value of the data series */,
  "data_unit" TEXT /* The unit of the data */,
  "data_description" TEXT /* A brief description of the data series */,
  "last_updated" TEXT /* The date when the data series was last updated */,
  "is_valid" INTEGER /* Whether the data series is valid and can be used for analysis */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("series_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_data_series_country_id FOREIGN KEY ("country_id") REFERENCES countries ("country_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_data_series_dataset_id FOREIGN KEY ("dataset_id") REFERENCES datasets ("dataset_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "data_points" (
  "point_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each data point */,
  "series_id" INTEGER /* ID of the series the point belongs to */,
  "year" INTEGER /* Year the data was collected */,
  "value" REAL /* Value of the data point */,
  "data_source_id" INTEGER /* The ID of the specific data source for this data point */,
  "data_unit" TEXT /* The unit of the data point */,
  "data_quality_score" INTEGER /* A score indicating the quality of the data point */,
  "last_updated" TEXT /* The date when the data point was last updated */,
  "is_valid" INTEGER /* Whether the data point is valid and can be used for analysis */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("point_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "employment_rates" (
  "rate_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each employment rate */,
  "country_id" INTEGER /* ID of the country the rate belongs to */,
  "gender" TEXT /* Gender the rate applies to (e.g., total, women, men) */,
  "year" INTEGER /* Year the data was collected */,
  "rate" REAL /* Employment rate */,
  "age_group" TEXT /* The age group the rate applies to (e.g., 15-64, 65+) */,
  "employment_type" TEXT /* The type of employment (e.g., full-time, part-time, self-employed) */,
  "last_updated" TEXT /* The date when the employment rate was last updated */,
  "is_valid" INTEGER /* Whether the employment rate is valid and can be used for analysis */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("rate_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_employment_rates_country_id FOREIGN KEY ("country_id") REFERENCES countries ("country_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "gdp_by_country" (
  "gdp_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each GDP data point */,
  "country_id" INTEGER /* ID of the country the GDP data belongs to */,
  "year" INTEGER /* Year the data was collected */,
  "gdp" REAL /* GDP of the country */,
  "gdp_per_capita" REAL /* GDP per capita */,
  "gdp_growth_rate" REAL /* Annual growth rate of GDP */,
  "last_updated" TEXT /* The date when the GDP data was last updated */,
  "is_valid" INTEGER /* Whether the GDP data is valid and can be used for analysis */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("gdp_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_gdp_by_country_country_id FOREIGN KEY ("country_id") REFERENCES countries ("country_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "population_by_country" (
  "population_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each population data point */,
  "country_id" INTEGER /* ID of the country the population data belongs to */,
  "year" INTEGER /* Year the data was collected */,
  "population" INTEGER /* Population of the country */,
  "age_distribution" TEXT /* The age distribution of the population */,
  "population_growth_rate" REAL /* Annual growth rate of the population */,
  "last_updated" TEXT /* The date when the population data was last updated */,
  "is_valid" INTEGER /* Whether the population data is valid and can be used for analysis */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("population_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_population_by_country_country_id FOREIGN KEY ("country_id") REFERENCES countries ("country_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "poverty_rates" (
  "rate_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each poverty rate */,
  "country_id" INTEGER /* ID of the country the rate belongs to */,
  "year" INTEGER /* Year the data was collected */,
  "rate" REAL /* Poverty rate */,
  "poverty_line" REAL /* The poverty line used for the rate calculation */,
  "poverty_type" TEXT /* The type of poverty (e.g., absolute, relative) */,
  "last_updated" TEXT /* The date when the poverty rate was last updated */,
  "is_valid" INTEGER /* Whether the poverty rate is valid and can be used for analysis */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("rate_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_poverty_rates_country_id FOREIGN KEY ("country_id") REFERENCES countries ("country_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "education_data" (
  "education_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each education data point */,
  "country_id" INTEGER /* ID of the country the education data belongs to */,
  "year" INTEGER /* Year the data was collected */,
  "educated_population" INTEGER /* Number of people with education */,
  "educational_level" TEXT /* The level of education (e.g., primary, secondary, tertiary) */,
  "education_type" TEXT /* The type of education (e.g., public, private) */,
  "last_updated" TEXT /* The date when the education data was last updated */,
  "is_valid" INTEGER /* Whether the education data is valid and can be used for analysis */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("education_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_education_data_country_id FOREIGN KEY ("country_id") REFERENCES countries ("country_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "health_data" (
  "health_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each health data point */,
  "country_id" INTEGER /* ID of the country the health data belongs to */,
  "year" INTEGER /* Year the data was collected */,
  "life_expectancy" REAL /* Life expectancy of the population */,
  "healthcare_expenditure" REAL /* Healthcare expenditure per capita */,
  "healthcare_coverage" TEXT /* The type of healthcare coverage (e.g., public, private) */,
  "last_updated" TEXT /* The date when the health data was last updated */,
  "is_valid" INTEGER /* Whether the health data is valid and can be used for analysis */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("health_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_health_data_country_id FOREIGN KEY ("country_id") REFERENCES countries ("country_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "inflation_rates" (
  "rate_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each inflation rate */,
  "country_id" INTEGER /* ID of the country the rate belongs to */,
  "year" INTEGER /* Year the data was collected */,
  "rate" REAL /* Inflation rate */,
  "inflation_type" TEXT /* The type of inflation (e.g., consumer price index, producer price index) */,
  "last_updated" TEXT /* The date when the inflation rate was last updated */,
  "is_valid" INTEGER /* Whether the inflation rate is valid and can be used for analysis */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("rate_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_inflation_rates_country_id FOREIGN KEY ("country_id") REFERENCES countries ("country_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "interest_rates" (
  "rate_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each interest rate */,
  "country_id" INTEGER /* ID of the country the rate belongs to */,
  "year" INTEGER /* Year the data was collected */,
  "rate" REAL /* Interest rate */,
  "interest_type" TEXT /* The type of interest rate (e.g., central bank, commercial bank) */,
  "last_updated" TEXT /* The date when the interest rate was last updated */,
  "is_valid" INTEGER /* Whether the interest rate is valid and can be used for analysis */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("rate_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_interest_rates_country_id FOREIGN KEY ("country_id") REFERENCES countries ("country_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "trade_data" (
  "trade_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each trade data point */,
  "country_id" INTEGER /* ID of the country the trade data belongs to */,
  "year" INTEGER /* Year the data was collected */,
  "exports" REAL /* Exports of the country */,
  "imports" REAL /* Imports of the country */,
  "trade_partner" TEXT /* The main trade partner for the country */,
  "trade_balance" REAL /* The trade balance (exports - imports) */,
  "last_updated" TEXT /* The date when the trade data was last updated */,
  "is_valid" INTEGER /* Whether the trade data is valid and can be used for analysis */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("trade_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_trade_data_country_id FOREIGN KEY ("country_id") REFERENCES countries ("country_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "username" TEXT /* Username of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "password" TEXT /* Password of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., admin, analyst) */,
  "first_name" TEXT /* First name of the user */,
  "last_name" TEXT /* Last name of the user */,
  "phone_number" TEXT /* Phone number of the user */,
  "last_login" TEXT /* The date when the user last logged in */,
  "is_active" INTEGER /* Whether the user account is active */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "access_logs" (
  "log_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access log */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user accessing the data */,
  "dataset_id" INTEGER /* ID of the dataset being accessed */,
  "access_date" TEXT /* Date the data was accessed */,
  "action" TEXT /* The action performed (e.g., view, edit, download) */,
  "ip_address" TEXT /* The IP address from which the access was made */,
  "user_agent" TEXT /* The user agent string of the device used */,
  "session_id" TEXT /* The session ID of the user */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("log_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_dataset_id FOREIGN KEY ("dataset_id") REFERENCES datasets ("dataset_id")
)
